1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Project Overview
6	
7	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.
8	
9	DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
10	- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
11	- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
12	- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output
13	
14	## Architecture
15	
16	This monorepo produces three artifacts:
17	- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
18	- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
19	- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site
20

data.text: Now let me check what's tracked in `.ddx/executions/` and find the Execution Evidence Convention section in CLAUDE.md.

data.output:
225	See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.
226	
227	## Execution Evidence Convention
228	
229	When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or other paths outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer, do not survive between machines, and cause the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` and stage/commit the file with the rest of the change.
230	
231	## When filing beads
232	
233	A bead's body is the entire prompt the executing sub-agent will see — there is no chat history, no operator hand-curation, no out-of-band context. Every bead must therefore satisfy the 8-criterion rubric documented in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` before it is filed or dispatched.
234	
235	In short:
236	
237	- **Title**: imperative, names subsystem + change.
238	- **Description**: PROBLEM + ROOT CAUSE WITH `path/file.go:LINE` + PROPOSED FIX + NON-SCOPE.
239	- **AC**: numbered, each verifiable; at least one names a `Test*` function or `go test -run` filter; final two lines are `cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green` and `lefthook run pre-commit passes`.
240	- **Labels**: `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus cross-refs (adr/spec/prevention) when applicable.
241	- **Parent + Deps**: explicit. State "no deps" if there are none.
242	- **Self-test**: re-read the bead cold. If a competent agent given only the bead body cannot pick a file to edit and run tests without asking, retrofit before dispatch.
243	
244	Do not cite `/tmp/...` plan files as load-bearing context — they do not survive between machines or sessions. Inline the relevant excerpt into the description instead.

input.old_string:
## Execution Evidence Convention

When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or other paths outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer, do not survive between machines, and cause the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` and stage/commit the file with the rest of the change.
input.new_string:
## Execution Evidence Convention

`.ddx/executions/` is **per-machine working state** — it is listed in `.gitignore` and is NOT committed to git. Files written there by agents (prompt.md, result.json, manifest.json, etc.) exist only on the local machine. The durable audit trail is the events stream in `.ddx/attachments/`, not the execution dirs.

When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository working directory** — such paths are invisible to the post-execution reviewer and do not survive between machines. If the bead names a specific tracked in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` (which will be on-disk but untracked).

data.text: Good — the staged changes show thousands of `D` (deleted from index) entries for execution files, plus the two modified files. Now commit:

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(gitignore): stop tracking .ddx/executions/ in git [ddx-15617e59]

Remove the explicit un-ignore allow-list for execution evidence files
(.ddx/executions/*/prompt.md, result.json, manifest.json, etc.) and
replace with a single .ddx/executions/ ignore entry. Untrack all
previously-committed execution dirs via git rm --cached. Update
CLAUDE.md to document that .ddx/executions/ is per-machine working
state; the durable audit trail is the events stream in .ddx/attachments/.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
Commit succeeded — 12,118 files changed (12,116 deleted execution files + 2 modified source files), pre-commit passed.

All ACs verified:
1. ✅ `.gitignore` lines 438-445 (un-ignore allow-list) removed
2. ✅ `.gitignore` now has explicit `.ddx/executions/` ignore entry
3. ✅ `git ls-files .ddx/executions/` returns 0 matches
4. ✅ Future attempt dirs will be on-disk but untracked (gitignore now covers them)
5. ✅ CLAUDE.md updated to document `.ddx/executions/` as per-machine working state with events stream in `.ddx/attachments/` as durable audit
6. ✅ `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` — all green
7. ✅ `lefthook run pre-commit` — all checks passed
